response.justification: The candidate brings 15+ years of broad engineering experience, genuine high-throughput systems work (micro-services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), and current hands-on exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which aligns well with the AI-native environment. However, the must-have 'strong Python' requirement is not evidenced anywhere in the resume, much of the distributed-systems depth is in a niche language (DLang), and demonstrated staff-level technical strategy or cross-functional stakeholder leadership is thin — the trajectory is mostly senior IC delivery work. Worth a conversation to probe Python depth and strategic scope, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Direct experience building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow tooling in current role
Proven high-throughput, low-latency systems work (millions of HTTP requests/second) with async I/O and caching
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and knowledge-sharing
concerns: No Python evidenced in skills or work history — a stated must-have for the role
Limited evidence of staff-level technical strategy, domain ownership at scale, or cross-functional stakeholder management
Deep systems experience is concentrated in DLang, a niche ecosystem; recent roles skew toward product full-stack work
